description: Update ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect the complete integrated pipeline with input validation. Refine the Mermaid diagram to show the ValidateInput Choice state, the validation failure path, and the complete end-to-end flow. Add a section describing the validation logic and how invalid inputs are handled.
steps: Step 1 - Update the 'Current State Diagram' Mermaid diagram in ARCHITECTURE.md to include the ValidateInput (Choice) state between WriteInitialRecord and ProcessAudio. Show the branching: valid extensions go to ProcessAudio, invalid extensions go to MarkFailed -> NotifyFailed.
Step 2 - Update the 'Orchestration Layer' section to document the ValidateInput Choice state, explaining what extensions are valid and how invalid files are rejected.
Step 3 - Update the 'Processing Layer' section to document that the Lambda handler is now wired to the Lambda runtime API and performs input validation (checking required fields and file extension).
Step 4 - Add or update a section describing the complete end-to-end flow from S3 upload through to SNS notification, highlighting both the success and failure paths including the new validation step.
Step 5 - Update the 'Implemented Components' table to reflect that the Lambda is now fully wired (not a skeleton) and that input validation is implemented.
Step 6 - Remove or update the 'Next milestone' note at the bottom of the Current State Diagram section since validation is now implemented.
acceptance_criteria: Mermaid diagram shows ValidateInput Choice state with valid/invalid branching
Documentation describes the validation logic (valid extensions: .mp3, .wav, .m4a, .ogg, .flac)
End-to-end flow section covers both success and failure paths
All text is consistent with the actual CDK code
findings: Documentation-only change. All tests pass (cached), cdk synth succeeds. Updated Mermaid diagram, Orchestration Layer, Processing Layer, added End-to-End Flow section, updated Implemented Components table, and removed the Next milestone note.
